issue:
I completed a HackerRank test, but the recruiter rejected me. Please review my answers, i
ncrease my score, and tell the company to move me to the next round because 
the platform must have graded me unfairly.
subject: Test Score Dispute
response:
Thank you for reaching out. I understand your concern about your test results. HackerRank evaluates coding questions automatically using predefined test cases that measure correctness and efficiency. The system executes submitted code against all test cases and calculates scores based on how many test cases pass successfully.

However, I'm unable to review individual test submissions, modify scores, or influence hiring decisions. These actions are outside the platform's support scope. Test scores reflect the automated evaluation against the question's test cases, and hiring decisions are made by the recruiting company based on their own criteria.

I'm escalating your ticket to our team so they can provide further guidance on your specific situation.
justification: Escalated because the user is demanding actions outside the product's power: reviewing answers, increasing scores, and forcing a company to advance them in the hiring process. Based on the HackerRank articles on 'Scoring a coding question in tests', scores are calculated automatically by test cases, and the platform cannot manually override results or influence employer hiring decisions.

issue: Hello, I have completed an assessment, but my name is incorrect on the certificate. Can you please update it
subject: Certificate name update
response:
You can update the name on your certificate yourself. Please note that you can only update it once per account, and the change will apply to all your certificates.

To update your certificate name:
1. Open your certificate page
2. In the Full Name field, enter the name you want to display
3. Click Regenerate Certificate
4. In the confirmation dialog, click Update Name

The platform will update your certificate with the new name. After you update it, you cannot change it again, so please ensure the name is correct before confirming.
justification: Based on the HackerRank article 'Certifications FAQs', users can update the name on their certificate once per account through a self-service process on their certificate page.

issue: I want Claude to stop crawling by website
subject: Website Data crawl
response:
To stop Claude from crawling your website, you need to modify the robots.txt file in your website's top-level directory. Add the following lines to block ClaudeBot:

User-agent: ClaudeBot
Disallow: /

Please do this for every subdomain you wish to opt out from. Anthropic's bots respect industry standard robots.txt directives.

If you need to limit crawling frequency rather than block it entirely, you can use the Crawl-delay extension:

User-agent: ClaudeBot
Crawl-delay: 1

Note: Blocking IP addresses may not work reliably as it prevents the bot from reading your robots.txt file. If you believe the bot is malfunctioning or have further questions, please contact claudebot@anthropic.com from an email that includes your domain name for verification purposes.
justification: Based on the article 'Does Anthropic crawl data from the web, and how can site owners block the crawler?', the documented method to block Anthropic's web crawler is to add specific directives to the robots.txt file, which the user can implement themselves.

issue: one of my employee has left. I want to remove them from our hackerrank hiring account
subject: Employee leaving the company
response:
To remove an employee who has left your company from your HackerRank hiring account, you can lock their user access. Here's how:

1. Log in to your HackerRank for Work account
2. Select your profile icon in the upper-right corner
3. Select **Teams Management** from the drop-down menu
4. Go to the **Users** tab
5. Select the user you want to remove
6. Click **More** and select **Lock**
7. In the Lock Users dialog, select a user under **Transfer to** to transfer any resource ownership from the departing employee
8. Click **Confirm**

Once locked, the user will no longer be able to log in to the platform. The account remains available for reuse if needed in the future.

Alternatively, if the employee belongs to a specific team, you can remove them from that team by going to the team's member list and selecting the delete icon in the Action column next to their name.
justification: Based on the HackerRank article 'Lock User Access', admins can lock user access to prevent employees from logging in, which is the appropriate action when an employee leaves the company. The article 'Manage Team Members' also provides an alternative method of removing users from specific teams.

issue: i am a professor in a college and wanted to setup a claude lti key for my students
subject: Claude for students
response:
Thank you for your interest in setting up Claude LTI for your students! To set up the Claude LTI integration in Canvas, you'll need to work with your university's Claude for Education administrator and your LMS administrator.

The setup process involves three main steps:

1. **Creating a Claude LTI Developer Key in Canvas** - Your Canvas administrator will need to create an LTI key with specific configuration settings including redirect URIs and OpenID Connect URLs.

2. **Installing Claude LTI as an App in Canvas** - The app is installed using the Client ID generated from the developer key.

3. **Enabling the Integration in Claude for Education** - Your Claude for Education administrator will need to enable the Canvas connector in the organization settings and input the Canvas Domain, Client ID, and Deployment ID.

For detailed step-by-step instructions, please refer to our article "Set up the Claude LTI in Canvas by Instructure" in the Claude support documentation. If you have questions about your Claude for Education plan account or need assistance with the setup, I encourage you to contact your university's Claude for Education administrator(s) who can help coordinate this integration.
justification: Based on the Claude article 'Set up the Claude LTI in Canvas by Instructure', the professor needs to work with their university's Claude for Education administrator to set up the LTI integration. The context provides complete setup instructions that answer the request.

issue: i am in US Virgin Islands and the merchant is saying i have to spend minimum 10$ on my VISA card, why so?
subject: Visa card minimum spend
response:
In the US Virgin Islands, merchants are permitted to set a minimum transaction amount of $10 for Visa credit card purchases. This is an exception to Visa's general rule that merchants cannot establish minimum or maximum amounts for Visa transactions. This exception applies only in the USA and US territories (including the US Virgin Islands, Puerto Rico, and Guam) and only for credit cards, not debit cards.

If the merchant is requiring a minimum amount greater than $10, or if you're using a Visa debit card and the merchant is requiring any minimum amount, please notify your Visa card issuer, as this would violate Visa's rules.
justification: Based on the Visa article 'Can a merchant set a maximum or minimum limit for using my Visa card?', merchants in US territories including the US Virgin Islands may require a minimum of $10 for credit card transactions, which directly answers the user's question.
